author: m.kalinin
content: CFIing an EIP on Svalbard is that fast because there is always a daylight?

author: m.kalinin
content: Can we have all the inputs to `engine_newPayload` as public inputs? I believe that if we add anything to that call in the future the guest program would need to be changed as well as if we were having `NewPayloadRequest` root. The only difference is that each time we extend a list of inputs to the `newPayload` we'd have to extend the guest program API call to incorporate a new input

author: taulepton_
content: We can but I think we still need to add beacon block root and blob versioned hashes to the accumulator / partial header as I suspect without them we would have a binding gap for the beacon block roots used to populate the system contract and the blob kzg commitments?

author: taulepton_
content: One could argue that if we have them as part of the accumulator and the CL checks accumulator consistency then what’s the purpose of the fields in engine API?

author: taulepton_
content: I described the partial header commitment as enshrining the engine API into the core protocol. Current EngineAPI consistency checks are local against a single block. Accumulator consistency check is global consistency across all ancestors.

author: taulepton_
content: If we want to introduce a new field to the engineAPI the I believe we would also need to add it to the accumulator for the ancestry consistency checks. Otherwise we have the same problem we’re trying to fix now?

author: taulepton_
content: Thinking out loud here

author: taulepton_
content: In this model you can consider the engineAPI consistency check as being moved from the EL to the CL and also using an accumulator for ancestry consistency

author: m.kalinin
content: If it's a new field to ExecutionPayload or envelope or bid then it shouldn't be added retrospectively?

author: taulepton_
content: I'm not sure if I fully understand the question but I think any new field added to engineAPI would be added to the partial header hash AT the fork boundary the field is added.

author: m.kalinin
content: yes, this is my understanding

author: taulepton_
content:
What are your thoughts regarding:
- adding parent beacon root and blob kzg commitments to the partial header
- redundancy of engine API fields

author: m.kalinin
content:
> adding parent beacon root and blob kzg commitments to the partial header

I think we should add them, like all other fields that must be checked by both layers

author: m.kalinin
content:
> redundancy of engine API fields
What redundancy?

author: m.kalinin
content: Like, if EL can parse blob kzg commitments it doesn't need to get them from the Engine API anymore?

author: taulepton_
content: yes

author: taulepton_
content: I suspect we can pass the execution payload and nothing else in the engineAPI

author: m.kalinin
content: I need to think into it

author: taulepton_
content: sure thing. Let me know your thoughts when you've had a moment to consider

author: m.kalinin
content: right now i would say that if blob kzg commitments and parent beacon root on CL doesn't match the same values on EL side then the new accumulator value would be different between the layers. Thus, seems like we don't need to pass these values

author: m.kalinin
content: Although, `parent_beacon_block_root` is a part of EL block and not included into `ExecutionPayload` so we have to pass it alongside

author: taulepton_
content: I was thinking that we would include parent_beacon_block_root in the partial header hash and then I believe there is an EL system transaction that sets the beacon block root included in every block so we would assert consistency of this parent beacon block root value with the partial header.

author: taulepton_
content: note: I need to look into code to verify this but I believe this is how it would work.

author: m.kalinin
content: If we include `expectedBlobVersionedHashes` and `executionRequests` into a new commitment/accumulator then they may not be passed to the EL, while `parentBeaconBlockRoot` will have to be anyway. There is no system contract where that root is coming from, there is a system call which takes that root and inserts it into a system contract storage to make it usable by dapps.

author: taulepton_
content: Could you elaborate on why `parentBeaconBlockRoot` has to be treated differently?

author: m.kalinin
content: because it's effectively a part of EL block while it's not a part of `ExecutionPayload`, EL will take this value from the call params and incorporate it into a block it unmarshalls from the `newPayload` param set

author: taulepton_
content: but by adding it the partial header commitment you essentially commit to it making it indirectly part of the `ExecutionPayload`?

author: m.kalinin
content: but it's gonna be a commitment and not the value itself, EL needs the value for the block execution

author: taulepton_
content: The actual value itself is part of the system call / system transaction in the EL block right? It must be coming from somewhere because the EL as it stands can already sync the finalized prefix without any info from the CL.

author: m.kalinin
content: EL gets it from p2p during the range sync as it's a part of EL block, with newPayload this value is coming from the param set

author: taulepton_
content: I don't follow. I think even with newPayload it is still part of the EL block (its just encoded in a system transaction that the CL cant read).

author: taulepton_
content: Before I say too much on this front I will interrogate the specs and an EL client to confirm.

author: nero_eth
content:
I put up a first draft for EELS here:
https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2770

author: taulepton_
content: As stated above we are missing parent beacon block root and the blob commitments in the partial header.

author: nero_eth
content: Not sure I follow, there are no blob commitments in today's EL block header except the blob gas things.

author: m.kalinin
content: It is part of the EL block but the EL cannot assemble that block with `ExecutionPayload` only, it needs `parent_beacon_block_root` as well

author: taulepton_
content: Ahhh I see, now I understand. thanks for clarifying

author: taulepton_
content: in absence of the blob commitments in the accumulator how do you confirm type3 blob transactions that are consistent with the CL are included in the blocks?

author: m.kalinin
content: blob commitments are passed to EL aside to each payload for verification, if we want decouple that verification we would need to include them in the new commitment

author: nero_eth
content: hmm I see, so same applies to parent_beacon_block_root, which we would also want in the partial header then?

author: taulepton_
content: I believe so, yes. I suspect we can replace the parent_hash which is the EL parent hash, with the parent_beacon_block_root.

author: nero_eth
content: I don't think they cover the same thing though

author: taulepton_
content: Possibly. Could you elaborate?

author: nero_eth
content: In my mental model, one chains the EL blockchain, the other chains the CL blockchain

author: nero_eth
content: Let me think this through for a sec

author: taulepton_
content: I think the CL blockchain already wraps the EL blockchain so you get indirect chaining

author: taulepton_
content: Do you think it makes sense to keep `ExecutionPayload` and `parent_beacon_block_root` decoupled or should we add `parent_beacon_block_root` to the `ExecutionPayload`?

author: nero_eth
content: under "100% dishonest" bid.block_hash can diverge from the actual EL block_hash. With only parent_beacon_block_root in the accumulator, you've committed to the CL claim but not to the EL actual, so a builder lie about bid.block_hash (claim points at H', EL imported P' with hash H') can be there with the accumulator still matching on the CL side. parent_hash in the accumulator forces bid.parent_block_hash == EL's actual block_hash.

author: m.kalinin
content: `parent_beacon_block_root` isn't in `ExecutionPayload` on CL side, this is the reason behind this field coming aside

author: taulepton_
content: Isn't that already captured in the pre-existing block_hash validity + chaining semantics? parent_hash is already part of the EL blocks and I'd assume that chain is asserted.

author: taulepton_
content: do you know the reason it's not currently inlcuded on the CL side? I suppose it would be simple to add.

author: m.kalinin
content: it's in the `BeaconBlock` structure already, including into `ExecutionPayload` on CL side will be unnecessary duplication

author: taulepton_
content: makes sense

author: jsign
content: so IIUC, if `parent_beacon_root` won't be part of `ExecutionPayload`, looks to me we can't use `ExecutionPayload` as the sole struct for the guest program input, right? i.e. we should keep using NewPayloadRequest

author: potuz
content: Why is this! As I said above this beacon block root is committed already in the hash chain

author: taulepton_
content: correct `ExecutionPayload` is missing the parent beacon block root.

author: taulepton_
content: If the CL only sees the EL parent block hash in the accumulator how does it know which parent beacon root was used in the EL block?

author: potuz
content: which check in the current CL block processing are you referring to?

author: taulepton_
content: The parent beacon block root is passed via engineAPI to the EL. The EL uses this to populate the system contract that stores beacon block roots.
